thinking.text: The output shows the script ran and produced results, but I see many zeros in the recovered matrix. That seems wrong. The output shows the end of the run with the stolen A1 matrix printed. Let me check the quality of recovery by comparing with actual A1.

tool_calls.Name: commands
tool_calls.Arguments:
{"keystrokes": "cd /app && python3 -c "
import numpy as np
from forward import A1

stolen = np.load('/app/stolen_A1.npy')
actual = A1

print('Stolen A1 shape:', stolen.shape)
print('Actual A1 shape:', actual.shape)

# Normalize rows
actual_norm = actual / np.linalg.norm(actual, axis=1, keepdims=True)
stolen_norm = stolen / np.linalg.norm(stolen, axis=1, keepdims=True)

# Compute absolute correlation matrix
corr = np.abs(stolen_norm @ actual_norm.T)
print('\nCorrelation matrix shape:', corr.shape)

# Greedy matching
print('\nMatching stolen rows to actual rows:')
used = set()
for i in range(len(stolen)):
    best_j = -1
    best_c = -1
    for j in range(len(actual)):
        if j not in used:
            c = abs(np.dot(stolen_norm[i], actual_norm[j]))
            if c > best_c:
                best_c = c
                best_j = j
    if best_j >= 0:
        used.add(best_j)
        print(f'  Stolen {i} -> Actual {best_j}: corr={best_c:.4f}')
    else:
        print(f'  Stolen {i} -> no match')
"
", "duration": 0.5}

detail.visible_answer: The script seems to have run and produced results. Let me verify the quality by comparing with the actual A1 matrix.

stolen_norm = stolen / np.linalg.norm(stolen, axis=1, keepdims=True)
